elements.name: circle
elements.description: The 'circle' element defines a circle based on a center point and a radius.

elements.name: ellipse
elements.description: The 'ellipse' element defines an ellipse which is axis-aligned with the current user coordinate system based on a center point and two radii.

notes: Partially supported, see [bug 12382004](https://developer.microsoft.com/microsoft-edge/platform/issues/12382004/).

elements.name: line
elements.description: The 'line' element defines a line segment that starts at one point and ends at another.

elements.name: metadata
elements.description: Metadata which is included with SVG content should be specified within 'metadata' elements. The contents of the 'metadata' should be elements from other XML namespaces, with these elements from these namespaces expressed in a manner conforming with the Namespaces in XML Recommendation [xml-names].

elements.name: polygon
elements.description: The 'polygon' element defines a closed shape consisting of a set of connected straight line segments.

elements.name: polyline
elements.description: The 'polyline' element defines a set of connected straight line segments. Typically, 'polyline' elements define open shapes.

elements.name: rect
elements.description: The 'rect' element defines a rectangle which is axis-aligned with the current user coordinate system. Rounded rectangles can be achieved by setting non-zero values for the 'rx' and 'ry' geometric properties.

elements.name: script
elements.description: A 'script' element is equivalent to the 'script' element in HTML and thus is the place for scripts (e.g., ECMAScript). Any functions defined within any 'script' element have a "global" scope across the entire current document.

elements.name: style
elements.description: The 'style element' element allows style sheets to be embedded directly within SVG content. SVG's 'style element' element has the same attributes as the corresponding element in HTML.

elements.name: symbol
elements.description: The 'symbol' element is used to define graphical templates which can be instantiated by a 'use' element but which are not rendered directly.

elements.name: text
elements.description: The 'text' element defines a graphics element consisting of text. The 'tspan' element within a 'text' or another 'tspan' element, allows one to switch the style and/or adjust the position of the rendered text inside the 'tspan' element relative to the parent element.

notes: Until Safari 6, textPath was not re-rendered when the referenced path was changed dynamically (see [bug 15799](https://webkit.org/b/15799))

elements.name: tspan
elements.description: The 'text' element defines a graphics element consisting of text. The 'tspan' element within a 'text' or another 'tspan' element, allows one to switch the style and/or adjust the position of the rendered text inside the 'tspan' element relative to the parent element.

notes: Before version 56, Firefox does not completely follow the `use` cascading rules (see [bug 265894](https://bugzil.la/265894)). A fix is [documented by Amelia Bellamy-Royds on StackOverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/27866893/svg-fill-not-being-applied-in-firefox/27872310#27872310).

attributes.name: alignment-baseline
attributes.description: This property is defined in the CSS Line Layout Module 3 specification. See 'alignment-baseline'. [css-inline-3]

attributes.name: autofocus
attributes.description: This content attribute allows authors to ask a focusable element to be focused after it's connected to a document. See autofocus in the HTML specification for details.

notes: Partially supported, see [bug 12382004](https://developer.microsoft.com/microsoft-edge/platform/issues/12382004/).
Partially supported, see [bug 12382004](https://developer.microsoft.com/microsoft-edge/platform/issues/12382004/).

attributes.name: baseline-shift
attributes.description: This property is defined in the CSS Line Layout Module 3 specification. See 'baseline-shift'. [css-inline-3]

attributes.name: class
attributes.description: The 'class' attribute assigns one or more class names to an element, which can then be used for addressing by the styling language.

notes: Safari incorrectly interprets `clip: auto` as `clip: rect(auto, auto, auto, auto)`.

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: See the CSS Color Module Level 3 specification for the definition of 'color'. [css-color-3]

attributes.name: color-interpolation
attributes.description: The SVG user agent performs color interpolations and compositing at various points as it processes SVG content. The 'color-interpolation' property controls which color space is used for the following graphics operations:

notes: Only the default value of `sRGB` is implemented
Only the default value of `sRGB` is implemented
Only the default value of `sRGB` is implemented

attributes.name: crossorigin
attributes.description: The crossorigin attribute is a CORS settings attribute, and unless otherwise specified follows the same processing rules as in HTML [HTML].

notes: Starting in Firefox 67, the maximum size allowed for custom cursors is 32x32 pixels due to cursors being misused by certain malicious sites.

attributes.name: cx
attributes.description: The 'cx' property describes the horizontal center coordinate of the position of the element.

attributes.name: cy
attributes.description: The 'cy' property describes the vertical center coordinate of the position of the element.

attributes.name: d
attributes.description: The 'd' property is used to specify the shape of a 'path' element.

attributes.name: direction
attributes.description: The property specifies the inline-base direction of a 'text' or 'tspan' element. It defines the start and end points of a line of text as used by the 'text-anchor' and 'inline-size' properties. It also may affect the direction in which characters are positioned if the 'unicode-bidi' property's value is either embed or bidi-override.

attributes.name: display
attributes.description: SVG uses two properties to toggle the visible display of elements that are normally rendered: 'display' and 'visibility'. Although they have a similar visible effect in static documents, they are conceptually distinct.

attributes.name: dominant-baseline
attributes.description: This property is defined in the CSS Line Layout Module 3 specification. See 'dominant-baseline'. [css-inline-3]

attributes.name: download
attributes.description: These attributes further describe the targetted resource and its relationship to the current document. Allowed values and meaning are as defined for the a element in HTML. The SVG Working Group is waiting on the HTML specification to clarify the processing model and algorithms for these attributes. The attributes should follow the definitions and algorithms as specified in the HTML specification. For more details on the ongoing clarification effort, see the HTML specification issue on processing model and algorithms for the HTML A element.

attributes.name: fill
attributes.description: The 'fill' property paints the interior of the given graphical element. The area to be painted consists of any areas inside the outline of the shape. To determine the inside of the shape, all subpaths are considered, and the interior is determined according to the rules associated with the current value of the 'fill-rule' property. The zero-width geometric outline of a shape is included in the area to be painted.

attributes.name: fill-opacity
attributes.description: 'fill-opacity' specifies the opacity of the painting operation used to paint the fill the current object. (See Painting shapes and text).

attributes.name: fill-rule
attributes.description: The 'fill-rule' property indicates the algorithm (or winding rule) which is to be used to determine what parts of the canvas are included inside the shape. For a simple, non-intersecting path, it is intuitively clear what region lies "inside"; however, for a more complex path, such as a path that intersects itself or where one subpath encloses another, the interpretation of "inside" is not so obvious.

notes: Before Firefox 40, `font-size-adjust: 0` was incorrectly interpreted as `font-size-adjust: none` ([bug 1144885](https://bugzil.la/1144885)).
A `font-stretch` definition must be added to the `@font-face` before this property will function.
Before Firefox 44, `oblique` was not distinguished from `italic`.

values.grammar: normal | none | [ <common-lig-values> || <discretionary-lig-values> || <historical-lig-values> || <contextual-alt-values> || stylistic(<feature-value-name>) || historical-forms || styleset(<feature-value-name> #) || character-variant(<feature-value-name> #) || swash(<feature-value-name>) || ornaments(<feature-value-name>) || annotation(<feature-value-name>) || -- [ small-caps | all-small-caps | petite-caps | all-petite-caps | unicase | titling-caps ] || <numeric-figure-values> || <numeric-spacing-values> || <numeric-fraction-values> || ordinal || slashed-zero || <east-asian-variant-values> || <east-asian-width-values> || ruby || [ sub | super ] ]

attributes.name: fr
attributes.description: New in SVG 2. Added to align with Canvas.

attributes.name: fx
attributes.description: 'fx', 'fy', and 'fr' define the start circle for the radial gradient. The gradient will be drawn such that the 0% gradient stop is mapped to the perimeter of this start circle.

attributes.name: fy
attributes.description: See 'fx'.

attributes.name: glyph-orientation-horizontal
attributes.description: This property has been removed in SVG 2.

attributes.name: glyph-orientation-vertical
attributes.description: This property applies only to vertical text. It has been obsoleted in SVG 2 and partially replaced by the 'text-orientation' property of CSS Writing Modes Level 3. The following SVG 1.1 values must still be supported by aliasing the property as a shorthand to 'text-orientation' as follows:

attributes.name: gradientTransform
attributes.description: Contains the definition of an optional additional transformation from the gradient coordinate system onto the target coordinate system (i.e., 'userSpaceOnUse' or 'objectBoundingBox'). This allows for things such as skewing the gradient. This additional transformation matrix is post-multiplied to (i.e., inserted to the right of) any previously defined transformations, including the implicit transformation necessary to convert from object bounding box units to user coordinate system.

attributes.name: gradientUnits
attributes.description: Defines the coordinate system for attributes 'x1', 'y1', 'x2' and 'y2'.

attributes.name: height
attributes.description: See the CSS 2.1 specification for the definitions of width and height.

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: The location of the referenced object, expressed as an URL reference. Refer to the common handling defined for URL reference attributes.

notes: Document fragments (including references to fragments in the current document) are not supported (see [bug 455986](https://bugzil.la/455986)).
Only accepts references to path elements. Basic shapes (rect, circle, ellipse, line, polygon, polyline) won't work.
Only accepts references to path elements. Basic shapes (rect, circle, ellipse, line, polygon, polyline) won't work.

attributes.name: hreflang
attributes.description: These attributes further describe the targetted resource and its relationship to the current document. Allowed values and meaning are as defined for the a element in HTML. The SVG Working Group is waiting on the HTML specification to clarify the processing model and algorithms for these attributes. The attributes should follow the definitions and algorithms as specified in the HTML specification. For more details on the ongoing clarification effort, see the HTML specification issue on processing model and algorithms for the HTML A element.

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Must reflect the element's ID [DOM]. The 'id' attribute must be unique within the node tree, must not be an empty string, and must not contain any whitespace characters.

attributes.name: image-rendering
attributes.description: The CSS Image Values and Replacement Content Module Level 4 may in the future redefine this property. In particular it should allow the choice between smoothing and keeping a pixelated look when upscaling.

notes: Before Edge 79, `BackgroundImage` and `BackgroundAlpha` were supported.

values.grammar: SourceGraphic | SourceAlpha | BackgroundImage | BackgroundAlpha | FillPaint | StrokePaint | <filter-primitive-reference>
SourceGraphic | SourceAlpha | BackgroundImage | BackgroundAlpha | FillPaint | StrokePaint | <filter-primitive-reference>

notes: Before Edge 79, `BackgroundImage` and `BackgroundAlpha` were supported.

values.grammar: SourceGraphic | SourceAlpha | BackgroundImage | BackgroundAlpha | FillPaint | StrokePaint | <filter-primitive-reference>
SourceGraphic | SourceAlpha | BackgroundImage | BackgroundAlpha | FillPaint | StrokePaint | <filter-primitive-reference>
SourceGraphic | SourceAlpha | BackgroundImage | BackgroundAlpha | FillPaint | StrokePaint | <filter-primitive-reference>
SourceGraphic | SourceAlpha | BackgroundImage | BackgroundAlpha | FillPaint | StrokePaint | <filter-primitive-reference>

attributes.name: inline-size
attributes.description: An example of using 'inline-size' for wrapping horizontal text.

attributes.name: lang
attributes.description: The 'lang' attribute specifies the primary language for the element's contents and for any of the element's attributes that contain text. Its value must be a valid BCP 47 language tag, or the empty string. Setting the attribute to the empty string indicates that the primary language is unknown. [BCP47].

attributes.name: line-height
attributes.description: SVG uses the 'line-height' property to determine the amount of leading space which is added between lines in multi-line text (both for horizontal and vertical text). It is not applicable to text on a path.

attributes.name: marker
attributes.description: The 'marker property' property sets values for the 'marker-start', 'marker-mid' and 'marker-end' properties. The value of the 'marker property' is used directly for all three of the corresponding longhand properties.

attributes.name: marker-end
attributes.description: The 'marker-start' and 'marker-end' properties are used to specify the marker that will be drawn at the first and last vertices of the given shape, respectively. 'marker-mid' is used to specify the marker that will be drawn at all other vertices (i.e., every vertex except the first and last). Possible values for 'marker-start', 'marker-mid' and 'marker-end' are:

attributes.name: marker-mid
attributes.description: The 'marker-start' and 'marker-end' properties are used to specify the marker that will be drawn at the first and last vertices of the given shape, respectively. 'marker-mid' is used to specify the marker that will be drawn at all other vertices (i.e., every vertex except the first and last). Possible values for 'marker-start', 'marker-mid' and 'marker-end' are:

attributes.name: marker-start
attributes.description: The 'marker-start' and 'marker-end' properties are used to specify the marker that will be drawn at the first and last vertices of the given shape, respectively. 'marker-mid' is used to specify the marker that will be drawn at all other vertices (i.e., every vertex except the first and last). Possible values for 'marker-start', 'marker-mid' and 'marker-end' are:

attributes.name: markerHeight
attributes.description: The 'markerWidth' and 'markerHeight' attributes represent the size of the SVG viewport into which the marker is to be fitted according to the 'viewBox' and 'preserveAspectRatio' attributes. A value of zero for either attribute results in nothing being rendered for the marker. A negative value for either attribute is an error (see Error processing).

attributes.name: markerUnits
attributes.description: The 'markerUnits' attribute defines the coordinate system for attributes 'markerWidth', 'markerHeight' and the contents of the 'marker element'. Values have the following meanings:

attributes.name: markerWidth
attributes.description: The 'markerWidth' and 'markerHeight' attributes represent the size of the SVG viewport into which the marker is to be fitted according to the 'viewBox' and 'preserveAspectRatio' attributes. A value of zero for either attribute results in nothing being rendered for the marker. A negative value for either attribute is an error (see Error processing).

attributes.name: media
attributes.description: This attribute specifies a media query that must be matched for the style sheet to apply. Its value is parsed as a media_query_list. If not specified, the style sheet applies unconditionally.

attributes.name: method
attributes.description: Indicates the method by which text should be rendered along the path.

attributes.name: offset
attributes.description: Indicates were the gradient stop is placed. For linear gradients, the 'offset' attribute represents a location along the gradient vector. For radial gradients, it represents a fractional distance from the edge of the innermost/smallest circle to the edge of the outermost/largest circle.

attributes.name: onabort
attributes.description: For every event type that the user agent supports, SVG supports that as an event attribute, following the same requirements as for event handler content attributes [HTML]. The event attributes are available on all SVG elements.

attributes.name: onafterprint
attributes.description: For every event type that the user agent supports, SVG supports that as an event attribute, following the same requirements as for event handler content attributes [HTML]. The event attributes are available on all SVG elements.

attributes.name: onbeforeprint

attributes.name: onbegin
attributes.description: Specifies some script to execute when "bubbling" or "at target" phase listeners for the corresponding event are fired on the element the attribute is specified on. See supported events table to determine which event each of these event attributes corresponds to. There are no restrictions on the values of this attribute.

attributes.name: ontimeupdate
attributes.description: For every event type that the user agent supports, SVG supports that as an event attribute, following the same requirements as for event handler content attributes [HTML]. The event attributes are available on all SVG elements.

attributes.name: ontoggle
attributes.description: For every event type that the user agent supports, SVG supports that as an event attribute, following the same requirements as for event handler content attributes [HTML]. The event attributes are available on all SVG elements.

attributes.name: onunload
attributes.description: For every event type that the user agent supports, SVG supports that as an event attribute, following the same requirements as for event handler content attributes [HTML]. The event attributes are available on all SVG elements.

attributes.name: onvolumechange
attributes.description: For every event type that the user agent supports, SVG supports that as an event attribute, following the same requirements as for event handler content attributes [HTML]. The event attributes are available on all SVG elements.

attributes.name: onwaiting
attributes.description: For every event type that the user agent supports, SVG supports that as an event attribute, following the same requirements as for event handler content attributes [HTML]. The event attributes are available on all SVG elements.

attributes.name: onwheel
attributes.description: For every event type that the user agent supports, SVG supports that as an event attribute, following the same requirements as for event handler content attributes [HTML]. The event attributes are available on all SVG elements.

attributes.name: opacity
attributes.description: See the CSS Color Module Level 3 for the definition of 'opacity'. [css-color-3]

attributes.name: orient
attributes.description: The 'orient' attribute indicates how the marker is rotated when it is placed at its position on the shape. Values have the following meanings:

attributes.name: overflow
attributes.description: See the Cascading Style Sheets Level 2 Revision 1 (CSS 2.1) Specification [CSS2] for the definition of 'overflow'.

attributes.name: paint-order
attributes.description: New in SVG 2. Added primarily to allow painting the stroke of text below its fill without needing to duplicate the 'text' element.

attributes.name: path
attributes.description: A path data string describing the path onto which the typographic characters will be rendered. If the attribute is not specified, or if the path data results in no valid path segments (e.g., the value is an empty string or an error occurs before any valid path segments), the path specified with 'href' is used instead. If the path data contains an error but produces at least one valid path segment before the error, the valid portion of the path is used following the rules in Error handling in path data.

attributes.name: pathLength
attributes.description: The author's computation of the total length of the path, in user units. This value is used to calibrate the user agent's own distance-along-a-path calculations with that of the author. The user agent will scale all distance-along-a-path computations by the ratio of 'pathLength' to the user agent's own computed value for total path length. 'pathLength' potentially affects calculations for text on a path, motion animation and various stroke operations.

attributes.name: patternContentUnits
attributes.description: Defines the coordinate system for the contents of the 'pattern'. Note that this attribute has no effect if attribute 'viewBox' is specified.

attributes.name: patternTransform
attributes.description: Contains the definition of an optional additional transformation from the pattern coordinate system onto the target coordinate system (i.e., 'userSpaceOnUse' or 'objectBoundingBox'). This allows for things such as skewing the pattern tiles. This additional transformation matrix is post-multiplied to (i.e., inserted to the right of) any previously defined transformations, including the implicit transformation necessary to convert from object bounding box units to user coordinate system.

attributes.name: patternUnits
attributes.description: Defines the coordinate system for attributes 'x', 'y', 'width' and 'height'.

attributes.name: ping
attributes.description: These attributes further describe the targetted resource and its relationship to the current document. Allowed values and meaning are as defined for the a element in HTML. The SVG Working Group is waiting on the HTML specification to clarify the processing model and algorithms for these attributes. The attributes should follow the definitions and algorithms as specified in the HTML specification. For more details on the ongoing clarification effort, see the HTML specification issue on processing model and algorithms for the HTML A element.

notes: This property is exposed but has no effect if the `browser.send_pings` preference is not set to `true`. See [bug 951104](https://bugzil.la/951104).
This property is exposed but has no effect if the `browser.send_pings` preference is not set to `true`. See [bug 951104](https://bugzil.la/951104).

attributes.name: pointer-events
attributes.description: For text elements, hit-testing is performed on a character cell basis:

attributes.name: points
attributes.description: The points that make up the polygon. All coordinate values are in the user coordinate system.

attributes.name: preserveAspectRatio
attributes.description: Indicates whether or not to force uniform scaling. Applies to all elements that establish a new SVG viewport (see elements that establish SVG viewports), plus the 'image', 'marker element', 'pattern' and 'view' elements

attributes.name: r
attributes.description: The 'r' property describes the radius of the 'circle' element.

attributes.name: refX
attributes.description: New in SVG 2: geometric keywords (matches use in 'symbol').

attributes.name: refY
attributes.description: New in SVG 2: geometric keywords (matches use in 'symbol').

attributes.name: referrerpolicy
attributes.description: These attributes further describe the targetted resource and its relationship to the current document. Allowed values and meaning are as defined for the a element in HTML. The SVG Working Group is waiting on the HTML specification to clarify the processing model and algorithms for these attributes. The attributes should follow the definitions and algorithms as specified in the HTML specification. For more details on the ongoing clarification effort, see the HTML specification issue on processing model and algorithms for the HTML A element.

attributes.name: rel
attributes.description: These attributes further describe the targetted resource and its relationship to the current document. Allowed values and meaning are as defined for the a element in HTML. The SVG Working Group is waiting on the HTML specification to clarify the processing model and algorithms for these attributes. The attributes should follow the definitions and algorithms as specified in the HTML specification. For more details on the ongoing clarification effort, see the HTML specification issue on processing model and algorithms for the HTML A element.

attributes.name: requiredExtensions
attributes.description: The value is a list of URL references which identify the required extensions, with the individual values separated by white space. Determines whether all of the named extensions are supported by the user agent. If all of the given extensions are supported, then the attribute evaluates to true; otherwise, the current element and its children are skipped and thus will not be rendered.

attributes.name: role
attributes.description: The 'role' attribute must be a set of space-separated tokens having values defined in Definition of Roles ([wai-aria], section 5.4).

attributes.name: rx
attributes.description: The 'rx' property describes the horizontal radius of the 'ellipse' element and the curve radius of the 'rect' element. When the computed value of 'rx' is auto, the used radius is equal to the absolute length used for 'ry', creating a circular arc. If both 'rx' and 'ry' have a computed value of auto, the used value is 0.

attributes.name: ry
attributes.description: The 'ry' property describes the vertical radius of the 'ellipse' element and the vertical curve radius of the 'rect' element. When the computed value of 'ry' is auto, the used radius is equal to the absolute length used for 'rx', creating a circular arc. If both 'rx' and 'ry' have a computed value of auto, the used value is 0.

notes: Partially supported, see [bug 12382004](https://developer.microsoft.com/microsoft-edge/platform/issues/12382004/).
Partially supported, see [bug 12382004](https://developer.microsoft.com/microsoft-edge/platform/issues/12382004/).

attributes.name: shape-rendering
attributes.description: The 'shape-rendering' property provides a hint to the implementation about what tradeoffs to make as it renders vector graphics elements such as 'path' elements and basic shapes such as circles and rectangles. Values have the following meanings:

attributes.name: side
attributes.description: Determines the side of the path the text is placed on (relative to the path direction). Specifying a value of right effectively reverses the path.

attributes.name: spacing
attributes.description: Indicates how the user agent should determine the spacing between typographic characters that are to be rendered along a path.

attributes.name: spreadMethod
attributes.description: Indicates what happens if the gradient starts or ends inside the bounds of the target rectangle.

attributes.name: startOffset
attributes.description: An offset from the start of the path for the initial current text position, calculated using the user agent's distance along the path algorithm, after converting the path to the 'textPath' element's coordinate system.

attributes.name: stroke
attributes.description: The 'stroke' property paints along the outline of the given graphical element.

attributes.name: stroke-dasharray
attributes.description: The 'stroke-dasharray' property controls the pattern of dashes and gaps used to form the shape of a path's stroke.

attributes.name: stroke-dashoffset
attributes.description: The 'stroke-dashoffset' property specifies the distance into the repeated dash pattern to start the stroke dashing at the beginning of the path. If the value is negative, then the effect is the same as dash offset d:

attributes.name: stroke-linecap
attributes.description: 'stroke-linecap' specifies the shape to be used at the end of open subpaths when they are stroked, and the shape to be drawn for zero length subpaths whether they are open or closed. The possible values are:

attributes.name: stroke-linejoin
attributes.description: 'stroke-linejoin' specifies the shape to be used at the corners of paths or basic shapes when they are stroked. For further details see the path implementation notes.

attributes.name: stroke-miterlimit
attributes.description: When two line segments meet at a sharp angle and a value of miter has been specified for 'stroke-linejoin', it is possible for the join to extend far beyond the thickness of the line stroking the path. The 'stroke-miterlimit' imposes a limit on the extent of the line join.

attributes.name: stroke-opacity
attributes.description: The 'stroke-opacity' property specifies the opacity of the painting operation used to stroke the current object. (See Painting shapes and text.) As with 'fill-opacity'.

attributes.name: stroke-width
attributes.description: This property specifies the width of the stroke on the current object. A zero value causes no stroke to be painted. A negative value is invalid. A <number> value represents a value in user units.

attributes.name: style
attributes.description: The 'style attribute' attribute is used to supply a CSS declaration of an element. The attribute is parsed as a declaration-list.

attributes.name: systemLanguage
attributes.description: The value is a set of comma-separated tokens, each of which must be a Language-Tag value, as defined in BCP 47 [BCP47].

attributes.name: tabindex
attributes.description: This content attribute allows authors to control whether an element is focusable, whether it is supposed to be reachable using sequential focus navigation, and what is to be the relative order of the element for the purposes of sequential focus navigation.

attributes.name: target
attributes.description: This attribute should be used when there are multiple possible targets for the ending resource, such as when the parent document is embedded within an HTML or XHTML document, or is viewed with a tabbed browser. This attribute specifies the name of the browsing context (e.g., a browser tab or an (X)HTML iframe or object element) into which a document is to be opened when the link is activated:

attributes.name: text-anchor
attributes.description: The 'text-anchor' property is used to align (start-, middle- or end-alignment) a string of pre-formatted text or auto-wrapped text where the wrapping area is determined from the 'inline-size' property relative to a given point. It is not applicable to other types of auto-wrapped text, see instead 'text-align'. For multi-line text, the alignment takes place for each line.

attributes.name: text-decoration
attributes.description: Text in SVG can be decorated with an underline, overline, and/or strike-through. The position and style of the decoration is determined respectively by the 'text-decoration-line' and 'text-decoration-style' properties, or by the 'text-decoration' shorthand property as defined in the Line Decoration section of the CSS Text Decoration Module Level 3 [(css-text-decor-3)] specification.

attributes.name: text-decoration-color
attributes.description: Text in SVG can be decorated with an underline, overline, and/or strike-through. The position and style of the decoration is determined respectively by the 'text-decoration-line' and 'text-decoration-style' properties, or by the 'text-decoration' shorthand property as defined in the Line Decoration section of the CSS Text Decoration Module Level 3 [(css-text-decor-3)] specification.

attributes.name: text-decoration-line
attributes.description: Text in SVG can be decorated with an underline, overline, and/or strike-through. The position and style of the decoration is determined respectively by the 'text-decoration-line' and 'text-decoration-style' properties, or by the 'text-decoration' shorthand property as defined in the Line Decoration section of the CSS Text Decoration Module Level 3 [(css-text-decor-3)] specification.

attributes.name: text-decoration-style
attributes.description: Text in SVG can be decorated with an underline, overline, and/or strike-through. The position and style of the decoration is determined respectively by the 'text-decoration-line' and 'text-decoration-style' properties, or by the 'text-decoration' shorthand property as defined in the Line Decoration section of the CSS Text Decoration Module Level 3 [(css-text-decor-3)] specification.

attributes.name: text-overflow
attributes.description: New in SVG 2. Added to allow user agents to handle text strings that overflow a predefined wrapping area in a more useful way. Aligns SVG and HTML/CSS text processing.

notes: Until Firefox 10, handling of `text-overflow` on blocks with inline overflow on both horizontal sides was incorrect. Before Firefox 10, if only one value was specified (such as `text-overflow: ellipsis;`), text was ellipsed on both sides of the block, instead of only the end edge based on the block's text direction.

attributes.name: text-rendering
attributes.description: The 'text-rendering' property provides a hint to the implementation about what tradeoffs to make as it renders text. Values have the following meanings:

notes: This property is only supported on Windows and Linux.

Initial versions had bugs on Windows and Linux that broke font substitution, small-caps, letter-spacing or caused text to overlap. See [bug 40156511](https://crbug.com/40156511), [bug 40430936](https://crbug.com/40430936), [bug 40444347](https://crbug.com/40444347), [bug 40286561](https://crbug.com/40286561).
This property is only supported on Windows and Linux.

Initial versions had bugs on Windows and Linux that broke font substitution, small-caps, letter-spacing or caused text to overlap. See [bug 40156511](https://crbug.com/40156511), [bug 40430936](https://crbug.com/40430936), [bug 40444347](https://crbug.com/40444347), [bug 40286561](https://crbug.com/40286561).
This property is only supported on Windows and Linux.

The `optimizeSpeed` option has no effect on Firefox 4 because the standard code for text rendering is already fast and there is not a faster code path at this time. See [bug 595688](https://bugzil.la/595688) for details.

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: This attribute specifies a title for the style sheet, which is used when exposing and selecting between alternate style sheets. The attribute takes any value.

attributes.name: transform
attributes.description: User agents must support the 'transform' property and presentation attribute as defined in [css-transforms-1].

notes: Does not work with `transform` SVG presentation attribute. Only works with `transform` CSS property. See [bug 201854](https://webkit.org/b/201854).

values.grammar: [ left | center | right | top | bottom | <length-percentage> ] | [ left | center | right | <length-percentage> ] [ top | center | bottom | <length-percentage> ] <length?> | [ [ center | left | right ] && [ center | top | bottom ] ] <length?>

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: These attributes further describe the targetted resource and its relationship to the current document. Allowed values and meaning are as defined for the a element in HTML. The SVG Working Group is waiting on the HTML specification to clarify the processing model and algorithms for these attributes. The attributes should follow the definitions and algorithms as specified in the HTML specification. For more details on the ongoing clarification effort, see the HTML specification issue on processing model and algorithms for the HTML A element.

attributes.name: vector-effect
attributes.description: These values can be enumerated. Thereby, the effect which has these characteristics simultaneously can be specified.

attributes.name: viewBox
attributes.description: The 'viewBox' attribute, in conjunction with the 'preserveAspectRatio' attribute, provides the capability to stretch an SVG viewport to fit a particular container element.

attributes.name: visibility
attributes.description: SVG uses two properties to toggle the visible display of elements that are normally rendered: 'display' and 'visibility'. Although they have a similar visible effect in static documents, they are conceptually distinct.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description: See the CSS 2.1 specification for the definitions of width and height.

attributes.name: writing-mode
attributes.description: The 'writing-mode' property gets defined by the CSS Writing Modes Module ([css-writing-modes-3]).

notes: Firefox 42 added support for bidirectional and RTL scripts in vertical modes.

attributes.name: x
attributes.description: The 'x' property describes the horizontal coordinate of the position of the element.

attributes.name: x1
attributes.description: The x- and y-axis coordinates of the start of the line.

attributes.name: x2
attributes.description: The x- and y-axis coordinates of the end of the line.

attributes.name: xlink:title
attributes.description: Deprecated attribute to describe the meaning of a link or resource in a human-readable fashion. New content should use a 'title' child element rather than a 'xlink:title' attribute.

element_values.element: a, image, linearGradient, pattern, radialGradient, script, textPath, use

attributes.name: xml:space
attributes.description: Deprecated XML attribute to specify whether white space is preserved in character data. The only possible values are the strings 'default' and 'preserve', without white space. Refer to the Extensible Markup Language (XML) 1.0 Recommendation [xml] and to the discussion white space handling in SVG.

attributes.name: y
attributes.description: The 'y' property describes the vertical coordinate of the position of the element.

attributes.name: y1
attributes.description: The x- and y-axis coordinates of the start of the line.

attributes.name: y2
attributes.description: The x- and y-axis coordinates of the end of the line.
